construct: Applies chemistry concepts and scientific skills in solving real life problems.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Classifies substances and interprets the physical and chemical changes of the substances for suitable application in life

subSkills.label: Classifies substances by nature/properties and interprets physical and chemical changes

elementsOfConstruct.description: Analyses and identifies chemical composition of substances for scientific decision making.

subSkills.label: Uses analytical procedures to identify chemical composition of solids, liquids or gases
subSkills.note: Skill-lens EoC focused on analytical identification methods; topic anchors provisional.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Evaluates industrial chemical processes and suggests alternative methods of production that are sustainable and environmentally safe

subSkills.label: Evaluates industrial process conditions and proposes sustainable alternatives

elementsOfConstruct.description: Applies Chemistry principles and mathematical operations to determine quantities of either reactants or products from quantitative (balanced) chemical equations in real-life situations or for solving problems

subSkills.label: Applies stoichiometric ratios and mole calculations from balanced equations
Determines theoretical yields and develops more efficient ways of increasing yields
subSkills.note: Skill-lens EoC on industrial yield investigation; content often overlaps EoC3/4.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Applies Chemistry principles in everyday life

subSkills.label: Designs solutions, products and services using chemistry knowledge for real-life situations
subSkills.note: Broad applied-chemistry EoC; provisional topic anchors.

components.structureNotes: SBA Form 2: 6 tasks/100 marks. All 6 EoC. Tests, assignments, practical, research.
SBA Form 3: 3 tasks/50 + 1 research project/50. All 6 EoC. Combined SBA 200 marks (30%).
6 compulsory scenario items, one per EoC1-6. Marks: 13,13,13,14,13,14. Duration 2h30. 70%.

rules: SBA: teachers develop scoring guides/rubrics per task aligned to syllabus competences.
Final Exam: ECZ-developed scoring guides for examiners.
Semi-structured items may require words, numbers, symbols, phrases, definitions, comparisons and calculations.
Open-ended responses must be complete sentences/paragraphs presented logically.
Where working is required, correct numerical answers without working earn no marks.

exemplars.scenarioTheme: Classifying substances and interpreting physical/chemical changes for suitable real-life application (per ECZ Chemistry exemplar EoC1)
